## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, this is Don Crowther with a video about the three audiences. So when it comes to marketing your webinar, your tactics of how you go take people through the funnel that leads them from initially getting exposed to the possibility of registering for your webinar all the way to buying your product, depending on one thing. And that is whether or not you have their email address. And when you think about it this way, you basically end up with three potential audiences. The first group is internal traffic, which I'm defining as you have their email address. So you could have had their email address from a year and a half ago when they signed up for an opt-in incentive that you have, or from 15 seconds ago when they opted into something and now you're showing them a webinar registration page. Remember that is this first group of people are people that you do have their email address. So you're able to communicate with them using email about the webinar itself. The second group is what I'm calling external traffic. These are people who you don't have their email address. I've got another slide in just a moment where I'll take you through the types of people who this can be, but add traffic, people who just come to your website, happen onto your page, those kinds of things. The third group is sort of a subgroup of that second group, which is they're coming from an affiliate. I'm calling an affiliate email. And these are people who you don't have their email address, but your affiliate does. And so there's a different strategy we use with this third group than we use with the second group in here. So going back to this middle box, external traffic, who are the people who you're marketing your webinar to, but you don't yet have their email address. So let's talk about this external traffic sources concept and who these people are. So basically, they are any source of people coming to your page other than from your email list. So this is people who come from your social media sites and the postings that you make there. It's people who come from a podcast, whether it's your podcast, maybe you're a guest on a podcast, or hey, this actually happened to me last week. Somebody referred to me on a pretty high traffic podcast. I wasn't on it. The person who was being who was interviewing them didn't really know me, but the person who was being interviewed referred to me. And all of us and I had this bump in traffic that came from a podcast that I had nothing literally nothing to do with.

## [03:03 - 06:07]

There's natural traffic that comes to your site coming from search engines or wherever else it comes to your site. That's external traffic to you. There's physical marketing, business cards, flyers, signage. I have a client who recently has created over million dollars in business from a sign that he placed in front of his physical office. Marketing his online product. So people are both going to the URL. It's a unique URL that's on this sign. And they're also coming into his physical office and saying, what is that? I want that thing. And so the physical people in the physical office are saying, hey, you should go to this URL and check it out. And he's built over a million dollars in business from putting that sign up in front of his office on a fairly busy street. But it's helped me to understand that those of us who have a online focus and everything we do, sometimes we may be missing out on huge opportunities from people who are putting physical things up in front of their offices and other places like that. Then there's ad traffic. So someone who sees an ad somewhere and clicks on that ad, you haven't got their email. And so their external traffic source. And then the final one is affiliate traffic. Now I break that out into a separate audience because we actually have an alternative way to get their email address. But all of these are external traffic sources that can be traffic to your offer, whatever offer that is. Now we send all three audiences to our webinar registration page. But the vast majority of them, 70% of them won't sign up for a webinar and we still want to sell to those people. So the key question is, how do you get the email address of these two audiences? Because your marketing to them is much easier and cheaper once you get that email audience. So I've already shown you this overview, bird's eye view of the funnel that we use to get people to buy from us through the entire webinar funnel. The challenge of this is that it starts with people already being on your list because all these other elements, if they don't sign up for the webinar, if they do, they go and you've got their email address and so you can go on. But if they don't, if you don't have their email address, you can't put them into the back end of the funnel. So how do we solve that? So here's what we do with people who are in the external group.

## [06:07 - 08:58]

So we start with the core funnel. And the reason I'm showing you this is because basically we replace the first part of that funnel. Everything else stays the same. So we start with external traffic, we then go to get send them to the webinar registration page. If they say yes, they go into the rest of the funnel. But if they say no, then we use a retargeting campaign to opt them in to get their email address. Then we deliver that opt in incentive, we promise. And then we end up putting them back into the funnel here at the replay promo because remember they've already said no, they don't want to attend the webinar. And so doesn't do any good to send them to the webinar attendance promo. We're going to put them in at the replay stage. We also have another strategy where we put an exit pop up on the webinar registration page, which does exactly this. It gets people to opt in and we can move forward with that. The challenge is we can only use that with certain audiences. Like we cannot use that if we're sending Facebook ads to that page because Facebook ads don't allow an exit pop up on the page. So that's the external promo sequence. As I said, everything in blue stays the same. You're just adding these green layers into it so that you can get their email address so that you can continue to market to them. All right, now let's look at the affiliate promo sequence. Same thing, we just take out those first two steps. We start with the affiliates sending an email, which then sends people to the webinar registration page. If they say, say yes, they go into the rest of the funnel. If they say no, then we have the affiliate making a second email campaign, which sends them to your opt in form. When they opt in, you then deliver that opt in and then we take them into the funnel at the replay promo. This the reason why this is here at this point in the program is I want to just open up to your open up your mind to the consideration that there are these other two groups besides the people who are on your email list. And to point out to you that we handle them differently, but we handle them very much the same. They go through the rest of the funnel, just like everybody else does. Except for this intermediate step, which is the step that is necessary to actually get their email address so that we can run them through the rest of the funnel.
